content.claim: A nationwide teacher mobilization in 2022-2023 created a union/parent/student coalition (October 2022 demonstrations drew 50,000+ participants) opposing government education policy, raising the political cost of any school reorganization.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Political feasibility lens
limitations: Mobilization was against wage/strike-rights policy, not specifically school closures
No evidence that parent/student networks can be redirected to oppose village closures specifically
Union infrastructure does not translate automatically to rural school defense
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
